sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content:
Cortex tracks three kinds of state for every node: the current content (hashes), the current staleness (computed snapshot), and verification status (human/agent judgment). But there is no unified record of how these states change over time.

Without a complete audit trail:
- Agents across conversations cannot tell what was already reviewed or how long something has been drifting.
- Staleness transitions are invisible — `compute_staleness` overwrites `nodes.staleness` on every run, erasing the previous value. You can see that a node is CLEAN now, but not that it was CONTENT_STALE for two weeks before someone fixed it.
- Doc edits via MCP tools (`cortex_update_section`, `cortex_write_doc`) leave no trace in history — an agent can rewrite an entire design spec and the history table shows nothing.
- Impact reports ("what changed in this PR?") require re-deriving transitions from raw CODE_ONLY/DESC_ONLY events rather than reading explicit state changes.

The history subsystem closes these gaps by recording every meaningful event — content changes, staleness transitions, verification actions, and doc edits — into an append-only log that survives across builds, conversations, and pruning cycles.

### Three tables, three responsibilities

| Table | Role | Write pattern | Read pattern |
|---|---|---|---|
| `node_history` | Audit trail — what happened over time | Append-only (events) | Ordered scan (impact reports, stale windows) |
| `node_verification` | Live verification state — is the current verification still valid? | Upsert (latest state) | Point lookup by node_id (staleness engine) |
| `nodes.staleness` | Computed snapshot — what needs attention right now? | Overwritten on build/check | Bulk read (viz, MCP, CLI) |

`node_history` is the only table that never gets recomputed or overwritten (except explicit pruning). `nodes.staleness` is recomputed from disk on every build/check. `node_verification` is upserted when a human or agent verifies. History and verification are written together in the same transaction so they cannot diverge.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-HI-01 — Automatic change recording**
As an agent, I want every content change (code body, docstring, doc section prose, heading) to automatically record a history row so that I can see exactly what changed and when without any manual tracking.

**US-HI-02 — Staleness transition recording**
As an agent, I want every staleness state change (CLEAN → CONTENT_STALE, CONTENT_STALE → VERIFIED, LINKED_STALE → CLEAN, etc.) to be recorded as a history event so that I can reconstruct the full lifecycle of a node — when it became stale, how long it stayed stale, and how it was resolved — without re-deriving transitions from hash comparisons.

**US-HI-03 — Verification audit trail**
As an agent, I want verification events to record who verified (human or agent), why (reason text), and the hash snapshot at verification time, so that I don't re-verify work a prior agent already reviewed and so the pre-push gate can flag agent-only verifications that need human sign-off.

**US-HI-04 — Link audit trail**
As a developer, I want every link addition and removal (`cortex_add_link`, `cortex_write_doc` link arrays, edge deletion during rebuild) to record a history event with the source node, target node, edge type, and actor — so that I can see how the graph structure changed over time. Without this, an agent can wire up the graph incorrectly and the history table shows nothing, leading to silent graph degradation.

**US-HI-05 — Impact report from history**
As a developer preparing a PR, I want `cortex report` to query history for all events since a reference point (last checkpoint, a git SHA, or a date) and produce a structured summary ordered by urgency — unverified nodes first, then agent-verified, then human-verified, new nodes, graph changes, and test results — so I can attach it to the PR as a review artifact. The same data powers the viz "since" filter for interactive drill-down.

**US-HI-06 — Stale window queries**
As an agent, I want to ask "how long has this node been stale?" and get a direct answer from history (BECAME_CONTENT_STALE event at T1, still no BECAME_CLEAN/VERIFIED event → stale for N days) rather than inferring it from the absence of DESC_ONLY events.

**US-HI-07 — Session/PR scoping**
As a developer, I want to scope history queries to a time range or commit range ("what happened since last checkpoint?" or "what changed between commit A and B?") so that impact reports reflect a specific unit of work, not the entire project lifetime. Checkpoints are semantic markers inserted via `cortex history checkpoint` — they record "this moment matters" and serve as named reference points for the "since" filter.

**US-HI-08 — Visual diff for stale nodes**
As a developer viewing a node in the viz, I want to see a side-by-side diff of the function's code (baseline vs current) in the source panel so that I can judge whether the docs need updating or the change was cosmetic — without leaving the viz or reading the full function. The baseline is retrieved via `git show {sha}:{file_path}` using a SHA from the node's history. Baseline resolution prefers verified/checkpoint rows, falls back to the oldest history row with a SHA (typically INITIAL), and can be overridden by clicking a specific SHA in the history tab. No source snapshots are stored in the database.

**US-HI-09 — Structured diff for agents**
As an agent reviewing staleness, I want a `cortex_diff(node_id)` MCP tool that returns the code delta since a baseline — old lines, new lines, change summary — so that I can update docs surgically based on what actually changed, rather than re-reading the entire function and guessing what's different. An optional `baseline_sha` parameter lets me diff against a specific commit from `cortex_history` output.

**US-HI-10 — Understand why code changed at a glance**
As a developer viewing a diff, I want to see the commit message that introduced the change so that I can understand *why* the code changed without leaving the viz to run `git log`. A bare SHA tells me nothing — the commit message tells me whether this was a refactor, a bug fix, or a feature, which directly informs whether the docs need updating. The commit message belongs in the diff view header (above the Monaco editor) where there is room for multi-line messages, not crammed into the history tab's compact table.

**US-HI-11 — Compare against any point in a node's history**
As a developer already viewing a diff, I want to pick which historical version I'm comparing against so that I can answer questions like "what changed since I last looked at this?" or "what did this function look like before the refactor two weeks ago?" The history tab's clickable SHAs are the secondary entry point for this — the primary flow is the "since" filter (US-HI-12) which sets the baseline for all nodes at once.

**US-HI-12 — See what changed across the project since a point in time**
As a developer preparing a PR or returning to a project after a break, I want to filter both the list view and graph view to show *only* nodes that changed since a reference point — a checkpoint, a date, or a SHA — so that I can focus my review on the delta, not the entire index. When I click a node in this filtered view, its diff should use the filter's reference SHA as the baseline, not the node's own verification history. This is the interactive drill-down counterpart to `cortex report` (US-HI-05), powered by the same `get_history_since()` infrastructure.

**US-HI-13 — Doc diff (word-level)**
As a developer viewing a doc node with the since filter active, I want a word-level diff of the document's sections (insertions highlighted in green, deletions in red, unchanged sections collapsible) between the baseline submodule commit and the current version, so I can see exactly what prose changed without reading both versions side-by-side. The diff resolves the docs submodule commit at the baseline SHA via `git ls-tree`, retrieves the old DocJSON via `git show` inside the submodule, and compares section content using jsdiff's `diffWords`. When viewing a section node, the diff scopes to just that section; the Full Doc toggle expands to all sections while staying in diff mode.

**US-HI-14 — Ghost nodes for deleted/purged entries**
As a developer using the since filter, I want nodes that were deleted since the baseline to appear as dimmed, strikethrough "ghost" rows in the list view with a red "deleted" badge, so I'm aware of what was removed — not just what was added or changed. Ghost nodes have no checkbox, no click handler, and no editor link, since the underlying code/doc no longer exists. The since endpoint returns ghost node metadata (title, type, location, tags, deleted_at) synthesized from preserved DELETED history rows.

**US-HI-15 — DELETED history event survives purge**
As a developer, I want the purge process to record a preserved `DELETED` snapshot in `node_history` before cascade-deleting a node, so that the since filter, impact reports, and `cortex report` can surface deletions instead of silently losing them. The DELETED row stores the node's last known title, type, subtype, location, and tags in its meta field. Only non-preserved history rows are deleted during cascade — the DELETED snapshot (`preserved=1`) survives indefinitely.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| `node_history` auto-recording on content change (upsert) | Done |
| `INITIAL` change type (first discovery) | Done |
| `CONTENT_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`, `CONTENT_AND_DESC` change types | Done (Phase 1 — renamed from `CODE_ONLY`/`CODE_AND_DESC`) |
| `AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED` change types | Done |
| `CHECKPOINT` change type (semantic marker, no pruning) | Done (Phase 4 — refactored to marker-only, removed `--keep`/`--older-than` flags) |
| `preserved=1` for verification and checkpoint rows (exempt from hard cap) | Done |
| `_HISTORY_ROW_LIMIT = 100` hard cap per node | Done |
| `cortex history checkpoint` command (semantic marker) | Done (Phase 4 — simplified to marker-only with `--message` support) |
| `cortex history agent-verified` command | Done |
| `cortex_history` MCP tool (agent access) | Done |
| `node_verification` table (live verification state) | Done |
| `record_staleness` wrapper (transition events + staleness column in one transaction) | Done (Phase 2) |
| Staleness transition events (`BECAME_CONTENT_STALE`, `BECAME_DESC_STALE`, `BECAME_LINKED_STALE`, `BECAME_STRUCTURAL_DRIFT`, `BECAME_CLEAN`) | Done (Phase 2) |
| `LINK_ADDED` / `LINK_REMOVED` structural events | Done (Phase 3) |
| `cortex report` CLI + `cortex_report` MCP tool (impact report from history) | Done (Phase 5) |
| VCS change IDs (git SHA) populated in all content-event history rows | Done (Phase 1) |
| Visual diff for stale nodes (viz source panel with Monaco diff editor) | Done (Phase 6 — US-HI-08) |
| `cortex_diff` MCP tool (structured diff for agents) | Done (Phase 6 — US-HI-09) |
| "Since" filter in viz list + graph views (show only changed nodes) | Done (Phase 7 — US-HI-12) |
| Commit message context in diff view header | Done (Phase 7 — US-HI-10) |
| Scoped diff baseline from "since" filter SHA | Done (Phase 7 — US-HI-11, US-HI-12) |
| Doc diff view (word-level jsdiff in viz doc panel) | Done (Phase 8 — US-HI-13) |
| `GET /api/docs/{id}/diff` endpoint (submodule-aware DocJSON diff) | Done (Phase 8 — US-HI-13) |
| `DELETED` history event with preserved snapshot before purge | Done (Phase 8 — US-HI-15) |
| Ghost nodes in since filter (deleted nodes rendered in list view) | Done (Phase 8 — US-HI-14) |
| `deleted_nodes` array in `/api/history/since` response | Done (Phase 8 — US-HI-14) |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
### Rename content-change types (prerequisite — trivial)

Rename `CODE_ONLY` → `CONTENT_ONLY` and `CODE_AND_DESC` → `CONTENT_AND_DESC` in `_derive_change_type` (db.py) and all consumers (`cortex_history` MCP tool, CLI output, viz history tab). The names now accurately describe what changed regardless of whether the node is a function body or a doc section's prose. `DESC_ONLY` is unchanged.

This is a rename only — no behavioral change. Requires `cortex init` on existing DBs (old change_type strings in history rows won't match new names, but history is reset on init anyway).

### `record_staleness` wrapper + staleness transition events (required for impact reports)

A new `record_staleness(db_path, project_root, nodes)` function wraps `compute_staleness` with the same signature. It becomes the single write point that all callers (CLI `cmd_check`, MCP `_compute_staleness_for_nodes`, viz `_compute_staleness_for_viz`) delegate to. It:

1. Reads old `nodes.staleness` values from the DB
2. Calls `compute_staleness(db_path, project_root, nodes)` (unchanged — pure computation, returns `dict[str, str]`)
3. Diffs old vs new for each node
4. Inserts transition events for every status change
5. Updates `nodes.staleness` column for all nodes

Steps 1–5 run in a single transaction. The existing thin wrappers in MCP and viz switch from calling `compute_staleness` to calling `record_staleness` — same args, same return type, but now transition events get written as a side effect.

**Transaction boundary with content events:** During `cortex build`, `upsert_node_conn` fires content events (`CONTENT_ONLY`, etc.) in transaction A, then `record_staleness` fires state events (`BECAME_CONTENT_STALE`, etc.) in transaction B. If the process crashes between A and B, the next `cortex build` recomputes everything from scratch — staleness and transition events are always derived from current disk state, not from prior events. This is acceptable.

**Dead code cleanup:** `_compute_staleness_from_history` in `viz/server.py` is dead code (nothing calls it). Remove it as part of this work.

New change types:

| Type | Trigger | Meta |
|---|---|---|
| `BECAME_CONTENT_STALE` | CLEAN/VERIFIED → CONTENT_STALE | `{"from": "CLEAN"}` |
| `BECAME_DESC_STALE` | CLEAN/VERIFIED → DESC_STALE | `{"from": "CLEAN"}` |
| `BECAME_LINKED_STALE` | CLEAN → LINKED_STALE | `{"from": "CLEAN", "linked_node": "..."}` |
| `BECAME_STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` | any → STRUCTURAL_DRIFT | `{"from": "CONTENT_STALE"}` |
| `BECAME_CLEAN` | any stale state → CLEAN | `{"from": "CONTENT_STALE"}` |

`AGENT_VERIFIED` and `MANUAL_VERIFIED` already cover the stale → VERIFIED transition. `BECAME_CLEAN` covers the case where staleness disappears because hashes re-aligned (e.g. a revert or organic code/doc realignment) — distinct from verification because nobody reviewed anything. This distinction lets `cortex report` differentiate "reverted/realigned" from "someone reviewed it."

All transition events use `preserved=0` — subject to the 100-row hard cap per node.

This is the prerequisite for US-HI-02 (staleness transition recording), US-HI-05 (impact reports), and US-HI-06 (stale window queries).

### Link events — structural audit trail

`LINK_ADDED` and `LINK_REMOVED` events recorded in `node_history` when edges are created or deleted. These are structural events — they don't change hashes and don't trigger staleness transitions, but they provide an audit trail for graph integrity.

Without link events, an agent can call `cortex_add_link` hundreds of times, wiring up the graph incorrectly, and the history table shows nothing. This leads to silent graph degradation. `cortex report` surfaces "N links added by agent:claude" as a review signal.

The history row is recorded on the source node (the doc section or test that gained/lost the edge). Meta contains both node IDs and the actor:

```json
{
  "edge_type": "documents",
  "source": "cortex::docs.architecture::database-layer",
  "target": "cortex::cortex.index.db::upsert_node_conn",
  "actor": "agent:claude"
}
```

Write points: `cortex_add_link` MCP tool, `cortex_write_doc` (link arrays in DocJSON), edge deletion during rebuild. All use `preserved=0`.

This addresses US-HI-04 (doc-side event recording — link additions are now auditable) and strengthens US-HI-05 (impact reports now include structural changes).

### ~~Doc-side content events~~ — Dropped

~~The MCP doc-editing tools need separate `DOC_UPDATED`/`DOC_CREATED` event types.~~

**Decision: not needed.** When `cortex_update_section` writes to disk and triggers a re-index, `upsert_node_conn` detects the hash change and records a `CONTENT_ONLY` or `DESC_ONLY` row on the doc section node. Impact reports distinguish code vs doc changes by checking the node's `subtype` (function vs docjson), not the change type. This eliminates the double-write deduplication problem the original design would have created.

### `cortex report` (primary deliverable)

Queries `node_history` for all events since the last checkpoint (or since a commit SHA / timestamp), joins with `node_verification` for current verification state and `test_results` for test outcomes. Produces structured markdown suitable for attaching to a PR.

Output template (urgency-ordered):

```markdown
# Cortex Impact Report
**Generated:** {timestamp}
**Since:** {reference point}
**Project:** {project name}

## Needs Attention (unverified stale nodes)
| Node | Status | Stale Since | File |
|---|---|---|---|

## Agent-Verified (needs human sign-off)
| Node | Verified By | Verified At | File |
|---|---|---|---|

## Human-Verified
| Node | Verified At | File |
|---|---|---|

## Graph Changes
- **Links added:** N (N by agent, N by system)
- **Links removed:** N

## Changes Summary
- **Code changes:** N nodes (N functions, N doc sections)
- **New nodes:** N
- **Stale windows opened:** N
- **Stale windows closed:** N (N via BECAME_CLEAN, N via verification)
```

The report answers:
- What nodes changed? (`CONTENT_ONLY`, `CONTENT_AND_DESC` events — distinguish code vs doc by joining on `nodes.subtype`)
- What was verified and by whom? (`AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED` events)
- What stale windows were opened and closed? (`BECAME_CONTENT_STALE` → `BECAME_CLEAN`/`VERIFIED` transitions)
- What is still stale right now? (nodes with `BECAME_*_STALE` and no subsequent `BECAME_CLEAN`/`VERIFIED`)
- What structural changes happened? (`LINK_ADDED`, `LINK_REMOVED` events with actor attribution)

Pure SQL read — deterministic, no LLM. Same data powers the viz list view "since" filter (US-LV-10) via `GET /api/impact`.

### `node_verification` table — keep vs absorb

The `node_verification` table stores the live verification state: who verified, reason, and the hash snapshot (`code_hash_at`, `desc_hash_at`) used by `compute_staleness` for VERIFIED promotion. History also records `AGENT_VERIFIED` / `MANUAL_VERIFIED` events but without the hash snapshot (it's high-volume, low-metadata).

**Decision: keep as a separate table.** The staleness engine needs fast point lookups of hash snapshots per node. Storing `code_hash_at`/`desc_hash_at`/`verified_by`/`reason` on every history row wastes space (95% of rows are CONTENT_ONLY/DESC_ONLY with no actor or reason). The two tables are written together in the same transaction by `verify_node` so they cannot diverge.

The relationship:
- `node_verification` = "is the current verification still valid?" (read by `compute_staleness`)
- `node_history` VERIFIED rows = "when was this verified, and was it an agent or human?" (read by `cortex report` and `agent-verified` gate)

### VCS change IDs in history rows

**Decision: git SHA only, populated at build time, best-effort retrieval.**

The `git_sha` column exists in `node_history` but is only populated for `CHECKPOINT` and verification rows today. During `cortex build`, call `get_git_sha()` once at the start and thread it through to `upsert_node_conn` so every content-change row (`INITIAL`, `CONTENT_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`, `CONTENT_AND_DESC`) records the HEAD SHA at build time. Structural events (`LINK_ADDED`, `LINK_REMOVED`) record HEAD at the time the edge is modified.

**Storage approach: no snapshots.** Git is already the snapshot store — every commit is a full copy of every file. The `git_sha` is a pointer into that archive. To retrieve old content: `git show {sha}:{file_path}`. No source content is stored in the database; no JSONB blobs, no duplication.

**Git complexity — explicit non-goals:**
- No branch tracking. History is a flat content timeline, not a VCS graph viewer. The `git_sha` records "HEAD at build time" regardless of which branch was checked out.
- No rebase handling. If a rebase rewrites history, old SHAs may become unreachable. `git show` will fail, and the diff endpoint returns "baseline unavailable." The history row itself — "this node changed at this time" — remains valid.
- No force-push recovery. Same graceful degradation as rebase.
- No merge-commit special casing. A merge SHA is just another SHA; `git show` works the same.

**Principle:** Cortex history is a content changelog, not a VCS history viewer. Git already has `git log --follow`, `git blame`, and branch graphs. Cortex records what happened to the node's content over time. The SHA is a best-effort pointer for retrieving old file content to enable diffs.

### Diff view — visual + MCP tool

Builds on VCS change IDs. When a node is CONTENT_STALE, the diff baseline comes from the most recent history row with `change_type IN ('AGENT_VERIFIED', 'MANUAL_VERIFIED', 'CHECKPOINT')` that has a non-NULL `git_sha`. This answers "what changed since docs were last verified."

**Backend:** `GET /api/nodes/{node_id}/diff` endpoint. Finds baseline SHA from history, runs `git show {sha}:{file_path}`, slices both old and current content to the node's `level_3_location` line range, returns `{old_content, new_content, baseline_sha, baseline_date}`. One subprocess call, no DB storage.

**Frontend:** "Diff" toggle button in the source panel (next to existing Focus button). Swaps `monaco.editor.create` for `monaco.editor.createDiffEditor` — Monaco handles side-by-side rendering, gutters, and inline mode out of the box. The History tab makes SHA entries clickable to show the diff at that specific point.

**`cortex_diff` MCP tool:** Same backend logic as the viz endpoint, returns structured text for agent consumption. Agent reads the delta and updates docs surgically instead of re-reading the full function. Returns `{node_id, baseline_sha, baseline_date, old_content, new_content, summary}` where `summary` is a line-count delta (e.g. "+5 / -2 lines in body"). Cheap to implement once the diff backend exists — thin MCP wrapper over the same `_get_node_diff()` helper.

**Graceful fallback:** If no baseline SHA exists (new node, never verified), or `git show` fails (SHA GC'd after rebase), the Diff button is disabled with tooltip "No baseline available," and `cortex_diff` returns `{"error": "no_baseline", "reason": "..."}`. Everything else still works. See list view PRD US-LV-13/14/15 for the full user stories.

### Checkpoint (semantic marker only)

`cortex history checkpoint` inserts a `CHECKPOINT` row with the current timestamp and HEAD git SHA (`preserved=1`). It is a semantic reference point — "this moment matters" — for the "since" filter in the viz (US-LV-10), `cortex report` (US-HI-05), and session scoping (US-HI-07). Typical usage: pre-release, post-merge, end of a work session.

No data is deleted. The original `--keep N` and `--older-than N` pruning flags have been removed. History rows are never deleted by user command — only the 100-row hard cap per node silently drops the oldest non-preserved rows as a safety valve.
